reasoning:
The question asks whether Waymo will feature in a Saturday Night Live skit in 2025. Searches confirming SNL content up to August 31, 2025, show there has not yet been a Waymo skit. Therefore, the resolution depends entirely on the remaining SNL episodes of 2025 (part of the Fall season, estimating about 8-10 episodes). 

Predicting a specific company or tech product will be parodied on SNL is inherently a low-probability event, especially when the service (Waymo) is still highly regional (mainly SF, LA, Phoenix, Austin) rather than a national everyday experience. While Waymo has occasionally had viral moments (e.g., the August 2024 honking parking lot incident or minor traffic jams), these haven't yet reached the level of broad cultural saturation that usually triggers an SNL sketch, unlike more ubiquitous brands like Uber or Tesla. 

The previous market prediction (0.14 as of August 21) was already quite low. Since then, no new events have significantly elevated Waymo's cultural footprint or likelihood of being featured. Factoring in the limited number of remaining episodes in 2025 and the decay of time without a skit, the probability should be somewhat lower than the previous market estimate. A 10% chance appropriately reflects the remaining window of opportunity.
